mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 2.28% suggests room for easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 5.0% provides baseline for deterioration detection
regime_hint: labor market stress or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity investors increase risk exposure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires coordinated demand signals and is not decomposable into independent effects.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 947k and RSAFS at 378.1B provide baseline for growth detection
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS show coordinated growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 1.5% over a 3-month period, signaling supply-side weakness or demand destruction that compresses corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 111.18 establishes baseline for decline measurement
regime_hint: supply shock or demand-driven production contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50k new jobs per month, signaling labor market cooling that reduces consumer spending power and equity earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 137.818M provides baseline for growth rate calculation
regime_hint: labor market deceleration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates by more than 0.5% month-over-month and the federal funds rate remains below 3%, the central bank faces policy constraints that create uncertainty about future rate paths. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice terminal rate expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 213.743 and FEDFUNDS at 2.28% define the regime
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns occur in < 58% of instances where both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle or recessionary conditions that compress equity risk premiums and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. This mechanism is always-on and does not require threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 11693.1B provides baseline for growth rate calculation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of periods with sub-1.5% annualized GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate by more than 15% quarter-over-quarter, signaling strong residential investment demand that drives forward earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 947k establishes baseline for acceleration measurement
regime_hint: housing cycle expansion or post-shock recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
